content: Just as the linguistic sense of reflexion as a core vocabulary for teaching high students structured critical thinking trains learners to spot flaws in their own writing and reasoning, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can borrow the structured vocabulary-focused reflexion framework from high school literacy curricula to format a LLM's internal self-critique into accessible, stepwise corrective prompts, rather than relying on unstructured open-ended reflection. This framework can be tested to reduce incoherent or off-track self-correction in LLMs solving complex reasoning tasks.
